title: [cross-file] lichen_l2_input can allocate net_pkt while holding rx_mutex causing priority inversion
description:
Interaction: lichen_l2.c:lichen_l2_input <-> Zephyr net_pkt pool

Bug: In lichen_l2_input() (lines 1656-1832), rx_mutex is acquired at line 1656 and held until line 1832. During this time, at line 1806-1808, net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer() is called with a timeout.

If the net_pkt pool is exhausted:
1. RX thread blocks up to CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS (default 50ms)
2. During this time, rx_mutex is held
3. Any other thread trying to acquire rx_mutex blocks
4. This includes lichen_peer_add/remove, lichen_l2_enable disable path

The code comment at line 1793-1804 acknowledges memory pressure but doesn't address the mutex hold duration.

Worse: the TX path also needs rx_mutex on enable/disable. If TX thread is waiting for lichen_l2_enable(false) which needs rx_mutex, but RX thread is blocked in alloc, and the net_pkt pool is waiting for TX to free packets, we have priority inversion or potential deadlock.

Trigger:
1. Net buf pool near exhaustion
2. RX frame arrives, RX thread enters lichen_l2_input
3. RX thread acquires rx_mutex (line 1656)
4. RX thread processes frame successfully
5. RX thread calls net_pkt_rx_alloc_with_buffer (line 1806)
6. Alloc blocks waiting for pool
7. Meanwhile, network stack tries to disable interface
8. lichen_l2_enable(false) tries to acquire tx_mutex then rx_mutex
9. tx_mutex acquired, rx_mutex blocks
10. If network stack holds resources needed to free net_pkts, deadlock
close_reason: Documented as known limitation with mitigations: added comment explaining priority inversion risk is mitigated by (1) Zephyr k_mutex priority inheritance, (2) CONFIG_LICHEN_L2_RX_ALLOC_TIMEOUT_MS bounds worst-case blocking, (3) low mutex contention probability at LoRa data rates. Added issue ID to existing mutex-during-allocation comment.
